description: docker-compose dev stack (postgres, redis, minio, fastapi, dagster-webserver, dagster-daemon, dagster-worker-cpu, dagster-worker-heavy, frontend) starts cleanly with a single command and all health checks pass
verification: Run `docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.dev.yml up -d` exits 0
GET http://localhost:8000/healthz returns 200 with {"status":"ok"}
GET http://localhost:3000/dagster_version returns 200
MinIO console is reachable at http://localhost:9001
psql -c 'SELECT 1' using configured credentials returns 1

description: Postgres baseline migration runs cleanly, creating all business tables defined in §4.1 (users, source_collection, source, document_variant, operator, recipe, dataset, run)
verification: Running the migration command exits 0 with no errors
`\dt` in psql lists all 8 expected tables
Re-running migration is idempotent (exits 0, no duplicate table errors)

description: MinIO buckets (sources, documents, documents_vlm, lance, datasets) are created automatically on first startup via an init container or startup script
verification: After `docker compose up`, `mc ls` lists all 5 expected buckets
Uploading a test file to `s3://sources/test.txt` via the MinIO SDK succeeds

description: FastAPI can reach Dagster GraphQL endpoint and the DagsterGateway abstraction returns the Dagster version without error
verification: GET http://localhost:8000/api/admin/dagster-status returns 200 with {"dagster_version": "..."}
Restarting only the fastapi container (dagster running) still returns 200

description: Dagster can run a hello-world job with a single op that logs a message, and the run completes with status SUCCESS
verification: Triggering the hello-world job via DagsterGateway returns a run_id
Polling GET /api/runs/{run_id} returns {"status": "success"} within 60 seconds

description: verify/checks.sh smoke script runs all baseline checks and exits 0 on a clean install
verification: `bash verify/checks.sh smoke` exits 0
The script checks: API health, DB connection, MinIO connectivity, Dagster connectivity

description: A single admin user record can be seeded into the users table via a one-time setup command; the user can obtain a JWT token via POST /api/auth/token
verification: Running the seed command creates exactly one row in the users table
POST /api/auth/token with correct credentials returns 200 with {"access_token": "...", "token_type": "bearer"}
POST /api/auth/token with wrong password returns 401

description: All non-public API routes require a valid JWT Bearer token; requests without a token receive 401
verification: GET /api/sources/collections without Authorization header returns 401
GET /api/sources/collections with a valid token returns 200
GET /api/sources/collections with an expired token returns 401

description: Create a source collection: POST /api/sources/collections creates a new source_collection row in Postgres and returns the new record
verification: POST /api/sources/collections {"name": "test-coll", "dataset_card_md": "desc"} returns 201 with {"id": <int>, "name": "test-coll"}
Row exists in source_collection table with matching name
POST with duplicate name returns 409

description: List source collections: GET /api/sources/collections returns a paginated list of all collections owned by the authenticated user
verification: After creating 3 collections, GET /api/sources/collections returns {"items": [...], "total": 3}
GET /api/sources/collections?limit=2 returns {"items": [<2 items>], "total": 3}

description: Upload a PDF source: POST /api/sources/upload stores the file in MinIO at s3://sources/{source_id}/original.pdf, writes a source row to Postgres with correct sha256 and storage_uri, and returns source_id
verification: POST /api/sources/upload with a valid PDF file and collection_id returns 201 with {"id": <int>, "storage_uri": "s3://sources/<id>/original.pdf"}
The file exists in MinIO at the returned storage_uri path
The source row in Postgres has sha256 matching sha256sum of the uploaded file
The source row has kind='file', mime_type='application/pdf'

description: After a successful source upload, FastAPI notifies Dagster: adds a dynamic partition key (src_{source_id}) to the 'sources' partition definition and reports a runtime asset materialization for the 'source' external asset
verification: After uploading a PDF, the Dagster GraphQL query for dynamic partitions on 'sources' includes the new partition key
The Dagster UI shows a materialization event for asset 'source' with the correct partition key
GET /api/runs listing after upload shows a run record with kind='upload' (or the source appears in partition list)

description: Get source details: GET /api/sources/{id} returns the full source record including storage_uri, sha256, size, mime_type, and collection_id
verification: GET /api/sources/{id} for an existing source returns 200 with all required fields populated
GET /api/sources/99999 returns 404

description: List sources in a collection: GET /api/sources/collections/{id}/sources returns paginated source records for that collection
verification: After uploading 3 PDFs to a collection, GET /api/sources/collections/{id}/sources returns {"items": [<3 items>], "total": 3}
Each item includes id, original_name, storage_uri, sha256, uploaded_at

description: Operator registry seed: a migration or seed script inserts the initial MinerU extractor operator row into the operator table with correct category, config_schema, and input/output_kind values
verification: After running the seed, SELECT * FROM operator WHERE name='mineru' returns 1 row with category='extractor', input_kind='source', output_kind='document'
The config_schema column is valid JSON

description: List operators: GET /api/operators?category=extractor returns all active operators in that category
verification: GET /api/operators?category=extractor returns 200 with an array containing the MinerU operator row
Each item includes id, name, version, category, config_schema
GET /api/operators?category=tagger returns operators with category='tagger'

description: Get operator detail: GET /api/operators/{id} returns the full operator record including config_schema, output_schema, and default_config
verification: GET /api/operators/{mineru_id} returns 200 with all fields including config_schema as a valid JSON Schema object
GET /api/operators/99999 returns 404

description: Trigger MinerU extraction: POST /api/runs with {asset: 'extract_mineru', source_ids: [...]} launches a Dagster backfill for the specified source partitions, creates a run record in Postgres, and returns the dagster_run_id
verification: POST /api/runs {"asset": "extract_mineru", "source_ids": [<id>]} returns 202 with {"dagster_run_id": "...", "run_id": <int>}
A row exists in the run table with kind='extract', status='pending' or 'running'
The Dagster UI shows a backfill launched for the 'extract_mineru' asset

description: MinerU extractor operator processes a PDF source, produces a valid DoclingDocument JSON at s3://documents/{source_id}/extract_mineru/doc.docling.json, and writes a document_variant row to Postgres
verification: After triggering extract_mineru for a source and waiting for the run to succeed, GET /api/sources/{source_id}/documents returns 1 variant with extractor_name='mineru'
The file s3://documents/{source_id}/extract_mineru/doc.docling.json exists in MinIO and is valid JSON
The document_variant row has is_canonical=true (first variant is auto-canonical)
The document_variant row has a non-null dagster_run_id

description: List document variants: GET /api/sources/{source_id}/documents returns all document_variant rows for that source
verification: After a successful extraction, GET /api/sources/{id}/documents returns array with 1 item containing extractor_name, extractor_version, storage_prefix, is_canonical, materialized_at
GET /api/sources/99999/documents returns 404

description: Set canonical document variant: POST /api/sources/{source_id}/documents/{extractor_name}/set-canonical updates is_canonical on the target variant and clears it on any previous canonical
verification: POST /api/sources/{id}/documents/mineru/set-canonical returns 200
SELECT * FROM document_variant WHERE source_id={id} AND is_canonical=true returns exactly 1 row with extractor_name='mineru'
The unique partial index on is_canonical enforces the one-canonical constraint

description: Render document preview: GET /api/documents/{variant_id}/render returns a markdown representation of the DoclingDocument for display in the UI
verification: GET /api/documents/{variant_id}/render returns 200 with Content-Type text/markdown and non-empty body
The rendered markdown contains text extracted from the source PDF
GET /api/documents/99999/render returns 404

description: Lance global chunks table is initialized with the correct Arrow schema (as defined in §4.2) on first use; the table is created at s3://lance/chunks/
verification: After system startup and first chunking run, `lance.dataset('s3://lance/chunks/').schema` contains all required fields including chunk_id, source_id, text, token_count, attr_quality_score, attr_lang_code, attr_minhash_signature
The table path is accessible via the MinIO SDK

description: Trigger chunking: POST /api/runs with {asset: 'chunks', source_ids: [...]} launches a Dagster backfill for the chunking asset on the specified partitions
verification: POST /api/runs {"asset": "chunks", "source_ids": [<id>]} returns 202 with {"dagster_run_id": "..."}
A run row is created with kind='chunk'
Dagster shows a backfill for 'chunks' asset launched

description: Chunking operator applies fixed-size chunking to the canonical DoclingDocument for a source and writes chunk rows to the Lance table with correct chunk_id naming, source_id, text, and token_count
verification: After chunking a 10-page PDF, SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Lance where source_id={id} returns > 0
Each row has chunk_id matching pattern '{source_id}_{seq}', non-null text, non-null token_count, augmented_from=null
All attr_* columns are null on newly inserted rows
producer_asset='chunks' on all rows for this source

description: LanceChunksIOManager row mode: writing new chunk rows deletes any existing rows for the same source_id + producer_asset combination before inserting, preventing duplicates on re-run
verification: Run chunking on a source, then run it again; final Lance row count for that source equals the count from a single run (not doubled)
No rows with duplicate chunk_id values exist in the table

description: Trigger quality tagger: POST /api/runs {asset: 'attr_quality', source_ids: [...]} launches the quality tagger Dagster backfill; on completion, attr_quality_score and attr_quality_provider columns are populated in Lance for the target source's chunks
verification: POST /api/runs {"asset": "attr_quality", "source_ids": [<id>]} returns 202
After run completes, SELECT attr_quality_score FROM lance_chunks WHERE source_id={id} returns non-null float values for all rows
attr_quality_provider is set to the LLM model name used (e.g., 'gpt-4o-mini')

description: quality_gpt4 tagger operator calls LLM through the LLMGateway (never directly), produces a float score between 0 and 1 per chunk, and updates only the attr_quality_score and attr_quality_provider columns in Lance (column mode, no row inserts)
verification: After running quality tagger, all chunk rows for the source have attr_quality_score between 0.0 and 1.0
No new rows were inserted into Lance (row count is unchanged)
The LLM call in the operator code uses ctx.llm.call() or equivalent gateway abstraction, not a direct SDK import
Running the tagger a second time does not fail; it overwrites the column values

description: lang_fasttext tagger operator populates attr_lang_code and attr_lang_confidence columns in Lance for all chunks of a source using fasttext language detection
verification: After triggering attr_lang tagger and waiting for success, SELECT attr_lang_code FROM lance_chunks WHERE source_id={id} returns non-null ISO 639-1 codes for all rows
attr_lang_confidence values are floats between 0 and 1
No new rows inserted (column mode only)

description: minhash_dedup tagger operator populates attr_minhash_signature, attr_minhash_cluster_id, and attr_minhash_is_head columns in Lance for all chunks
verification: After triggering attr_minhash tagger, SELECT attr_minhash_signature FROM lance_chunks WHERE source_id={id} returns non-null list values for all rows
attr_minhash_is_head is true for exactly one chunk per duplicate cluster
No new rows inserted (column mode only)

description: LanceChunksIOManager column mode: tagger writes update only the tagger's own columns; other columns (including other attr_* columns) are left unchanged
verification: Run quality tagger, note attr_lang_code is null; run lang tagger; verify attr_quality_score is still set and attr_lang_code is now set
The column merge uses merge_insert on chunk_id key, not a full row replacement

description: Chunk query endpoint: POST /api/chunks/query with {filter, columns, limit, offset} executes a DataFusion SQL filter on the Lance table and returns matching chunks with a total count
verification: POST /api/chunks/query {"filter": "source_id = <id>", "limit": 10} returns {"items": [<up to 10 chunks>], "total": <N>}
POST /api/chunks/query {"filter": "attr_quality_score > 0.8"} returns only chunks meeting the filter
POST /api/chunks/query {"filter": "1=0"} returns {"items": [], "total": 0}

description: Chunk aggregate endpoint: POST /api/chunks/aggregate with {filter, group_by, metrics} returns grouped statistics (e.g., count per lang_code)
verification: POST /api/chunks/aggregate {"filter": "source_id = <id>", "group_by": "attr_lang_code", "metrics": ["count"]} returns array like [{"attr_lang_code": "zh", "count": 42}, ...]
Result is consistent with direct Lance count for the same filter

description: Chunk distribution endpoint: POST /api/chunks/distribution with {filter, column} returns a histogram of values for a numeric or categorical attribute column
verification: POST /api/chunks/distribution {"filter": "source_id=<id>", "column": "attr_quality_score"} returns {"buckets": [{"range": [0,0.1], "count": ...}, ...], "column": "attr_quality_score"}
POST /api/chunks/distribution {"column": "attr_lang_code"} returns categorical counts

description: Get single chunk: GET /api/chunks/{id} returns the full chunk record including text, token_count, source_refs, docling_refs, all attr_* columns, and augmented_from
verification: GET /api/chunks/{valid_chunk_id} returns 200 with all expected fields
GET /api/chunks/nonexistent-id returns 404

description: Chunk lineage endpoint: GET /api/chunks/{id}/lineage returns the lineage chain: the source record, document_variant record, and the chunk's producer_asset, producer_version, and augmented_from chain
verification: GET /api/chunks/{id}/lineage returns 200 with {"chunk": {...}, "source": {"id": ...}, "document_variant": {"id": ...}, "lineage_chain": [...]}
For a non-augmented chunk, lineage_chain has 1 entry; for an augmented chunk it traces to the root original chunk

description: Create a recipe: POST /api/recipes with a valid recipe definition object creates a recipe row in Postgres and returns the new record with id
verification: POST /api/recipes {"name": "my-sft", "definition": {"sources": [...], "filter": {...}, "schema": {"template": "sft_synthesis_qa", "config": {...}}, ...}} returns 201 with {"id": <int>, "name": "my-sft"}
Row exists in recipe table
POST with duplicate name returns 409

description: List recipes: GET /api/recipes returns all recipes owned by the authenticated user
verification: After creating 2 recipes, GET /api/recipes returns {"items": [<2 items>], "total": 2}
Each item includes id, name, description, created_at, updated_at

description: Get recipe: GET /api/recipes/{id} returns the full recipe including the definition JSON
verification: GET /api/recipes/{id} returns 200 with all fields including the definition object
GET /api/recipes/99999 returns 404

description: Update recipe: PUT /api/recipes/{id} updates the recipe definition and bumps updated_at; update is rejected if a dataset has been materialized from this recipe (schema frozen post-publish rule)
verification: PUT /api/recipes/{id} {"definition": {<updated>}} returns 200 with updated definition
updated_at timestamp is newer than before the update
After materializing a dataset from this recipe, PUT /api/recipes/{id} returns 422 or 409 with an appropriate error message

description: Recipe preview: POST /api/recipes/{id}/preview generates 3-5 sample output rows using the current recipe definition without writing to MinIO, and returns them as JSON
verification: POST /api/recipes/{id}/preview returns 200 with {"samples": [<3-5 items>]} where each item matches the schema template's expected output shape
For sft_synthesis_qa template, each sample has at minimum 'instruction' and 'output' fields
Preview completes in under 30 seconds for 3 samples

description: Materialize dataset: POST /api/datasets/{recipe_id}/materialize creates a dataset row (status=pending), launches a Dagster backfill for the dataset asset, and returns the dataset_id and dagster_run_id
verification: POST /api/datasets/{recipe_id}/materialize returns 202 with {"dataset_id": <int>, "dagster_run_id": "..."}
A dataset row exists in Postgres with status='pending', recipe_snapshot containing the frozen recipe definition
Dagster shows a backfill launched for the 'dataset' asset

description: sft_synthesis_qa materializer operator reads chunks from Lance using the recipe's filter, calls LLM via gateway to synthesize Q+A pairs, and writes train/validation Parquet splits to s3://datasets/{dataset_id}_v{version}/data/
verification: After materialization completes, Parquet files exist in MinIO at the expected path
Loading the Parquet file with pandas: columns include 'instruction' and 'output' (or as specified by the template)
README.md and recipe.json exist alongside the Parquet files
The recipe_snapshot in recipe.json matches the recipe definition at time of materialization
All LLM calls in the materializer use ctx.llm.call(), no direct SDK imports

description: HFDatasetIOManager writes materialized Parquet files to MinIO in a HuggingFace-compatible layout including README.md and dataset_infos.json, and updates the dataset row in Postgres to status=done with sample_count and size_bytes
verification: After materialization, GET /api/datasets/{id} returns {"status": "done", "sample_count": <N>, "size_bytes": <bytes>}
s3://datasets/{dataset_id}_v{version}/dataset_infos.json exists and is valid JSON
s3://datasets/{dataset_id}_v{version}/README.md exists and contains the dataset_card_md content

description: List datasets: GET /api/datasets returns all datasets with their status, recipe_id, version_tag, sample_count, and size_bytes
verification: After a successful materialization, GET /api/datasets returns array containing the new dataset with status='done'
Each item includes id, recipe_id, version_tag, status, sample_count, size_bytes, materialized_at

description: Get dataset detail: GET /api/datasets/{id} returns the full dataset record including recipe_snapshot and stats
verification: GET /api/datasets/{id} returns 200 with all fields including recipe_snapshot (the frozen recipe JSON), stats, and hf_repo_uri
GET /api/datasets/99999 returns 404

description: Dataset download: GET /api/datasets/{id}/download streams the Parquet files from MinIO as a zip archive or returns a presigned URL for direct download
verification: GET /api/datasets/{id}/download returns 200 with appropriate Content-Type and non-empty body (or a JSON with presigned_url field)
Downloading and extracting the result yields valid Parquet files loadable by pandas
GET /api/datasets/99999/download returns 404

description: Run status endpoint: GET /api/runs/{id} returns the run record including dagster_run_id, kind, status, started_at, ended_at, and config
verification: GET /api/runs/{id} returns 200 with all expected fields
GET /api/runs/99999 returns 404

description: List runs: GET /api/runs returns paginated run records, filterable by status; runs are ordered by started_at descending
verification: After triggering 3 runs, GET /api/runs returns {"items": [...], "total": 3}
GET /api/runs?status=success returns only completed runs
GET /api/runs?status=running returns only in-progress runs

description: Dagster event webhook: Dagster posts run status change events to a FastAPI webhook endpoint; FastAPI updates the corresponding run row in Postgres
verification: After a Dagster run completes, GET /api/runs/{id} shows status='success' or 'failed' (not permanently 'running')
The run's ended_at timestamp is set after completion

description: WebSocket run subscription: connecting to /api/ws/runs and subscribing to a run_id receives status change events when the run transitions (e.g., pending -> running -> success)
verification: Connect to ws://localhost:8000/api/ws/runs with a valid JWT
Trigger a run; receive at minimum one {"type": "run.status_changed", "run_id": "...", "to": "success"} message within 2 minutes
Disconnecting does not cause server errors

description: WebSocket notification subscription: /api/ws/notifications delivers asset.materialized events when a Dagster asset completes materialization
verification: Connect to /api/ws/notifications
Trigger an extraction run; receive a {"type": "asset.materialized", "asset_key": "extract_mineru", "partition_key": "src_..."} message
Receive a {"type": "chunks.added", "source_id": <id>, "count": <N>} message after chunking completes

description: LLM Gateway: all LLM calls in operators/materializers go through a centralized LLMGateway that reads API keys from environment, applies rate limiting, and logs call metadata (model, tokens, estimated cost) to Postgres or logs
verification: Triggering quality tagger generates log entries showing model name and approximate token count
Setting an invalid API key in env causes LLM calls to fail with a clear error (not an unhandled exception)
No direct openai.ChatCompletion or anthropic.Anthropic() calls exist outside the gateway code path (grep check)

description: DoclingDocIOManager writes DoclingDocument JSON, associated images, and manifest.json to MinIO in the correct path layout (s3://documents/{source_id}/extract_{name}/), handling partial failures atomically
verification: After a successful extraction, MinIO at s3://documents/{source_id}/extract_mineru/ contains doc.docling.json and manifest.json
If the MinIO write fails mid-way, no partial document_variant row is written to Postgres (or it is cleaned up)
manifest.json contains source_refs and version info as described in §4.3

description: Login page: the frontend renders a login form; submitting valid credentials obtains a JWT and redirects to the home/dashboard page; invalid credentials show an error message
verification: Navigating to http://localhost:5173/login renders a form with email and password fields
Submitting valid credentials redirects to /
Submitting invalid credentials shows 'Invalid credentials' error message without navigating away

description: Protected routes: all frontend pages except /login redirect to /login when no valid JWT is present in local storage
verification: Navigating to /sources without a token redirects to /login
After logout (clearing token), navigating to /datasets redirects to /login

description: Sources page: lists all source collections; each collection is expandable to show its sources with name, size, and upload date; includes an Upload button
verification: After creating a collection and uploading a PDF via the UI, the Sources page shows the collection with 1 source
The source row displays original_name, size in human-readable format, and uploaded_at date
Clicking the Upload button opens a file picker

description: Source upload flow in UI: user selects a PDF file, optionally enters a collection name, clicks upload; a progress indicator is shown; on success the new source appears in the list
verification: Selecting a PDF and clicking Upload shows a loading state
On success, the new source appears in the Sources list without a full page reload
On upload failure, an error toast is shown

description: Source detail page: navigating to a source shows its metadata and a Trigger Extraction button; after extraction, shows the document variant with a Preview button
verification: Navigating to /sources/{id} shows source name, size, sha256, upload date
Clicking 'Trigger Extraction' sends POST /api/runs and shows a run status indicator
After extraction completes, a document variant entry appears with a Preview button

description: Document preview page: clicking Preview on a document variant renders the markdown output of GET /api/documents/{variant_id}/render in a readable format
verification: Navigating to the preview opens a page or modal with rendered markdown content
Headings, paragraphs, and lists are formatted visually
The rendered content is non-empty for a real PDF source

description: Chunks Explorer page: shows a filter sidebar with quality score slider, language code checkboxes, and source collection checkboxes; main area shows a virtual-scroll table of matching chunks with live count
verification: Navigating to /chunks renders the filter sidebar and table
Moving the quality score slider updates the chunk count within 2 seconds without a full page reload
Checking/unchecking a language code updates the table
The count label shows 'N chunks match'
Table supports at least 1000 rows visible without freezing (virtual scroll)

description: Chunk detail panel: clicking a row in the Chunks Explorer slides out a detail panel showing the chunk's text, token_count, all attribute values, source_refs (page/bbox), and a link to the source document
verification: Clicking a chunk row opens a side panel with chunk text and attr_quality_score, attr_lang_code values
source_refs shows at minimum page number
A link or button navigates to the source document

description: Distribution tab in Chunks Explorer: switching to the Distribution tab shows a histogram for attr_quality_score and a bar chart for attr_lang_code based on the current filter
verification: Clicking the Distribution tab renders at least 2 charts
Each histogram/bar chart shows data consistent with the current filter
Clicking a histogram bucket adds a filter pill to the active filters

description: Trigger tagger from Chunks Explorer: clicking 'Apply Tagger' opens an operator selection dropdown; selecting a tagger and clicking Run triggers POST /api/runs; a status toast shows progress
verification: Clicking 'Apply Tagger' opens a modal with a list of available taggers from GET /api/operators?category=tagger
Selecting a tagger and clicking Run sends POST /api/runs and shows a toast with the run_id
The toast updates to 'completed' when the WebSocket delivers a run.status_changed event

description: Recipes page: lists all recipes with name, description, and created_at; includes a Create New Recipe button that navigates to the recipe editor
verification: After creating a recipe via the API, /recipes page lists it
Clicking Create New Recipe navigates to /recipes/new
Each row has an Edit button that navigates to /recipes/{id}/edit

description: Recipe editor: a multi-step form (Sources, Filter, View, Schema, Sampling, Output) allows the user to build a recipe; saving sends PUT /api/recipes/{id} or POST /api/recipes; the recipe definition is persisted
verification: All 6 steps render without errors
Step 1 shows available source collections (loaded from GET /api/sources/collections)
Step 4 shows available schema templates as selectable cards (loaded from GET /api/operators?category=materializer)
Clicking Save sends the correct request and shows a success message
After saving, GET /api/recipes/{id} returns the updated definition

description: Recipe editor auto-generated config form: when a schema template (materializer operator) is selected in Step 4, the config form is auto-generated from the operator's config_schema JSON Schema field
verification: Selecting the sft_synthesis_qa template renders form fields for synthesizer_model (dropdown) and prompt_template (textarea)
Required fields are marked and form submission is blocked if they are empty
Changing the template selection changes the form fields

description: Recipe editor filter builder: Step 2 provides a visual filter builder that lets users add conditions on chunk attributes (quality_score range, lang_code in set, source collection); the built filter is serialized to a SQL WHERE clause
verification: Adding a quality_score > 0.7 condition via the UI produces filter {"where": "attr_quality_score > 0.7"} in the recipe definition
Adding a language filter produces valid SQL like "attr_lang_code IN ('zh', 'en')"
The filter pill appears in the filter bar and can be removed

description: Datasets page: lists all datasets with name (from recipe), version_tag, status, sample_count, and size_bytes; completed datasets have a Download button
verification: After a successful materialization, /datasets page shows the dataset with status='done' and sample_count > 0
Clicking Download initiates a download of the Parquet files
Pending/running datasets show a status badge without a download button

description: Dataset detail page: clicking a dataset shows its recipe_snapshot, stats (split sizes, attribute distributions), and a list of Parquet file paths
verification: Navigating to /datasets/{id} renders the recipe_snapshot as formatted JSON or YAML
Stats section shows train/validation split sizes
A file listing shows the Parquet files stored in MinIO

description: Materialize dataset button: the dataset detail or recipe detail page has a Materialize button; clicking it calls POST /api/datasets/{recipe_id}/materialize and shows a progress indicator connected to the WebSocket
verification: Clicking Materialize shows a loading spinner/badge
When the WebSocket delivers a run.status_changed to='success' event, the status badge updates to 'Done'
If materialization fails, the badge shows 'Failed'

description: Activity/Runs page: lists recent runs with dagster_run_id, kind, status, started_at, and ended_at; links to Dagster UI for log details
verification: Navigating to /runs shows a list of run records
Each row displays kind (extract/chunk/tag/materialize), status badge, and timestamps
A 'View Logs' link opens the Dagster UI run page for that dagster_run_id in a new tab

description: Real-time run status updates in UI: the Runs page and any page with an active run shows live status updates via WebSocket without requiring a page refresh
verification: With /runs open, trigger a new extraction; the new run appears in the list within 5 seconds without refreshing
The status badge changes from 'running' to 'success' or 'failed' automatically when the run completes

description: OpenAPI spec is generated automatically by FastAPI and is accessible at GET /api/openapi.json; the spec covers all routes defined in §9.1
verification: GET /api/openapi.json returns 200 with a valid OpenAPI 3.x JSON object
The spec includes paths for /api/sources/upload, /api/chunks/query, /api/recipes, /api/datasets, /api/runs, /api/operators, /api/ws/runs
All request/response schemas have types (no plain {}).

description: TypeScript API types are generated via `make codegen` from the OpenAPI spec and committed in packages/api-types/; the web frontend imports types only from that package
verification: Running `make codegen` exits 0 and updates packages/api-types/
The frontend TypeScript build (`pnpm build`) succeeds after codegen
grep -r 'from.*api-types' apps/web/src/ returns results (frontend imports from the package)
No raw fetch calls in apps/web/src use manually typed response shapes

description: Async SQLAlchemy: all database interactions in apps/api/ use the async session (AsyncSession); no synchronous session.query() calls exist anywhere in the API codebase
verification: grep -r 'session.query\|Session()\|sessionmaker(' apps/api/ returns 0 results
Running the full test suite does not produce 'greenlet_spawn' or 'sync session' warnings
All async db operations complete under normal load without blocking the event loop

description: Source SHA-256 deduplication: attempting to upload a file with the same sha256 as an existing source in the same collection returns 409 Conflict with the existing source_id; the file is not written to MinIO twice
verification: Upload a PDF; note the sha256; upload the same file again to the same collection; receive 409 with {"existing_source_id": <id>}
MinIO contains only one copy of the file
Uploading the same file to a different collection succeeds (dedup is per-collection)

description: Run logs link: GET /api/runs/{id}/logs returns either the Dagster logs for that run or a redirect URL to the Dagster UI run page
verification: GET /api/runs/{id}/logs for a completed run returns 200 with log content or {"dagster_ui_url": "http://..."}
GET /api/runs/99999/logs returns 404

description: Operator registration endpoint: POST /api/operators (admin only) inserts a new operator row into Postgres; the new operator becomes available via GET /api/operators
verification: POST /api/operators with a valid operator payload returns 201 with {"id": <int>}
GET /api/operators?category=tagger subsequently includes the new operator
POST /api/operators with duplicate name+version returns 409

description: Orphan data cleanup job: a scheduled Dagster job or cron task detects MinIO files that have no corresponding Postgres record (orphaned from partial writes) and deletes them; runs on a schedule
verification: Manually inserting a test file into MinIO without a DB record, then running the cleanup job, results in the file being deleted
The cleanup job logs how many orphaned files were removed

description: Source upload metadata.json: alongside the original file in MinIO, a metadata.json is written containing mime_type, original_name, sha256, size, uploaded_at, and license
verification: After uploading a PDF, s3://sources/{source_id}/metadata.json exists in MinIO
Its content parses as valid JSON with all required fields matching the source row

description: Recipe schema validation: POST /api/recipes and PUT /api/recipes/{id} validate the recipe definition against the Recipe DSL schema (§7.1); invalid definitions are rejected with 422 and descriptive error messages
verification: POST /api/recipes with a definition missing required 'sources' field returns 422 with an error pointing to the missing field
POST /api/recipes with an invalid filter SQL string returns 422
POST /api/recipes with a valid definition returns 201

description: Recipe materialization versioning: each call to POST /api/datasets/{recipe_id}/materialize increments the version_tag; the recipe_snapshot is frozen at the time of the call; subsequent recipe edits do not affect the snapshot
verification: Materialize a recipe; note version_tag='v1'; update the recipe; materialize again; new dataset has version_tag='v2'
The recipe_snapshot on the v1 dataset still contains the original recipe definition, not the updated one
GET /api/datasets/{v1_id} returns the original recipe_snapshot

description: Environment config: all secrets and external endpoints (POSTGRES_URL, REDIS_URL, MINIO_ENDPOINT, DAGSTER_GRAPHQL, OPENAI_API_KEY) are read from environment variables; no secrets are hardcoded in source code
verification: grep -r 'OPENAI_API_KEY\s*=\s*["\x27]sk-' apps/ dagster/ plugins/ returns 0 results
Starting the stack with missing required env vars causes a clear startup error (not a runtime exception on first request)
All configurable values are documented in docker/docker-compose.dev.yml or a .env.example file

description: API error responses: all FastAPI endpoints return structured JSON error responses with {"detail": "...", "code": "..."}; unhandled exceptions return 500 with a generic message (no stack traces in production)
verification: GET /api/sources/99999 returns {"detail": "Source not found", "code": "not_found"}
An intentionally triggered 500 in a test route returns {"detail": "Internal server error"} without a Python traceback in the body

description: verify/checks.sh api layer check: runs targeted API smoke tests (create collection, upload source, query chunks) and exits 0 on a correctly deployed system
verification: `bash verify/checks.sh api` exits 0 after a clean stack startup
The script creates a collection, uploads a test PDF, and queries GET /api/sources/collections

description: verify/checks.sh pipeline layer check: triggers a full extraction + chunking run on a test fixture PDF, waits for completion, and verifies chunks exist in Lance
verification: `bash verify/checks.sh pipeline` exits 0
The check confirms chunk rows exist in Lance for the test source after the pipeline completes

description: Source upload supports size limit: files exceeding a configured maximum size (default 500 MB) are rejected with 413 before being written to MinIO
verification: Uploading a file > 500 MB returns 413 with {"detail": "File too large"}
No partial file is written to MinIO for the rejected upload

description: Source file type validation: POST /api/sources/upload rejects non-PDF files with 422 (in MVP, only PDF is supported per §12.1)
verification: Uploading a .txt file returns 422 with message indicating only PDF is supported
Uploading a .docx file returns 422
Uploading a valid .pdf file returns 201

description: Dagster Postgres schema separation: the Dagster internal schema and the business schema coexist in the same Postgres instance under separate schema namespaces (e.g., public and dagster); migrations for each are independent
verification: \dn in psql lists at least two schemas: 'public' (business) and 'dagster' (or equivalent)
Running business migrations does not touch the dagster schema
Dagster webserver starts without errors when the dagster schema is initialized

description: Tagger seed: a seed script inserts the three MVP tagger operator rows (quality_gpt4, lang_fasttext, minhash_dedup) into the operator table with correct config_schema and output_schema values
verification: After running the seed, SELECT name FROM operator WHERE category='tagger' returns 3 rows: 'quality_gpt4', 'lang_fasttext', 'minhash_dedup'
Each operator's config_schema is a valid JSON Schema document
Each operator's output_schema lists the Lance columns it will write

description: sft_synthesis_qa materializer seed: a seed script inserts the sft_synthesis_qa materializer operator row into the operator table with a config_schema defining synthesizer_model and prompt_template fields
verification: SELECT name FROM operator WHERE category='materializer' AND name='sft_synthesis_qa' returns 1 row
config_schema includes 'synthesizer_model' as an enum and 'prompt_template' as a required string

description: Dagster asset factory: at startup, the Dagster code location loads all active operators from the operator table and generates corresponding Dagster asset definitions dynamically; a new tagger row in the DB becomes a new Dagster asset after code location reload
verification: After inserting a new test tagger operator row and calling the Dagster code location reload endpoint, GET /api/operators?category=tagger returns the new operator AND the Dagster UI shows a corresponding attr_* asset
Removing an operator (is_active=false) and reloading removes the asset from Dagster UI

description: Structured logging: all services log in JSON format with at minimum {timestamp, level, service, trace_id, message}; errors include exception type and message
verification: Tail the fastapi container logs; all lines parse as valid JSON
Triggering a 404 produces a log line with level='warning' and the request path
An intentional error produces a log with level='error' and exception details

description: Delete source: DELETE /api/sources/{id} removes the source row from Postgres, deletes the MinIO object at storage_uri, and cascades to delete associated document_variant rows
verification: DELETE /api/sources/{id} returns 204
GET /api/sources/{id} subsequently returns 404
The MinIO object no longer exists
SELECT * FROM document_variant WHERE source_id={id} returns 0 rows (cascade)

description: Delete source collection: DELETE /api/sources/collections/{id} removes the collection and cascades to delete all its sources and their document variants; rejects if any dataset references chunks from this collection
verification: DELETE /api/sources/collections/{id} with no dependent datasets returns 204
All source and document_variant rows for the collection are removed
Attempting to delete a collection with an active dataset materialization returns 409

description: Home / dashboard page: shows a summary of recent activity (last 5 runs), count of source collections, total chunks, and count of materialized datasets
verification: Navigating to / renders counts for source collections, chunks, and datasets
Recent runs section shows the 5 most recent run records with status badges
Numbers are consistent with API responses from GET /api/sources/collections, GET /api/chunks/aggregate, and GET /api/datasets

description: End-to-end smoke test: a scripted test uploads a fixture PDF, triggers extraction, chunking, and quality+lang tagging, creates a recipe, materializes a dataset, and downloads it; the entire flow completes without error
verification: Running `bash verify/checks.sh e2e` exits 0 within 10 minutes on a clean stack
The downloaded Parquet file is valid and contains at least 1 row
No error-level log messages appear in any service during the run

description: Augmenter support: LanceChunksIOManager row mode supports augmenter operators that write new chunk rows with augmented_from populated and all attr_* columns set to null
verification: After running a translate augmenter, new rows exist with augmented_from pointing to original chunk_ids
All attr_* columns on augmented rows are null

description: Docling extractor: extract_docling asset processes docx/pptx/html sources and produces a DoclingDocument JSON variant
verification: Uploading a .docx file and triggering extract_docling produces a document_variant with extractor_name='docling'
The resulting doc.docling.json is valid

description: Firecrawl extractor: extract_firecrawl asset fetches a URL source and produces a DoclingDocument JSON variant
verification: Uploading a URL source and triggering extract_firecrawl produces a valid document_variant
The document contains extracted text from the URL

description: VLM document enrichment: the document_vlm_enriched asset calls VLM API via LLMGateway for images in a DoclingDocument, replacing image placeholders with text descriptions
verification: After running document_vlm_enriched, the enriched document JSON contains non-empty image description fields
The VLM call uses ctx.llm.call()

description: Lineage UI: a Lineage page renders a react-flow DAG showing the asset graph for a selected source, with nodes for source, document, chunks, attributes, and dataset
verification: Navigating to /lineage?source_id={id} renders a graph with at least 4 connected nodes
Clicking a node shows metadata about that asset

description: Recipe raw YAML mode: the recipe editor has a toggle to switch between the visual multi-step form and a raw YAML text editor (monaco-editor); changes in YAML mode sync back to the visual form
verification: Toggling to YAML mode renders the recipe definition as YAML in a monaco editor
Editing the YAML and toggling back to visual mode reflects the changes

description: Advanced sampling: the recipe materializer supports stratified sampling per source collection with per_source_caps as defined in the Recipe DSL §7.1
verification: A recipe with per_source_caps: {1: 100} produces a dataset where at most 100 samples come from collection 1
stratified strategy produces proportional representation of sources
